_doc: Ordered list of Circuit Tutor tasks. Each task has: id, type (measure|problem|scenario|exploration), topicId, topicName, difficulty, data. Measure tasks pre-load a scaffold circuit with locked component ids. Edit this file to add/remove/reorder tasks.

data.brief: Complete the circuit so the ammeter reads the current through the resistor. Then type the reading below.
data.hint: The ammeter must be in series with R1. Form one complete loop.
data.explanation: I = V / R = 6 V / 3 Ω = 2 A.

data.brief: Connect the cell and voltmeter so V1 reads the p.d. across the bulb. Then type the reading.
data.hint: A voltmeter goes in parallel across the component you want to measure.
data.explanation: With only one bulb in the loop the full supply p.d. sits across it: 6 V.

data.brief: Finish the series loop of two resistors (2 Ω and 4 Ω) and an ammeter. What current does A1 read?
data.hint: In series, add the resistances. Then use I = V / R_total.
data.explanation: R_total = 2 + 4 = 6 Ω. I = 6 / 6 = 1 A. In series the current is the same everywhere.

data.brief: Wire the two resistors in parallel across the cell, with the ammeter measuring the TOTAL current from the cell. Enter A1's reading.
data.hint: The supply p.d. sits across each branch. Total I = I₁ + I₂.

data.question: A 6 V cell is connected in a series loop with a 2 Ω resistor. What is the current in the circuit?
workingSteps: Apply Ohm's law: V = I × R, so I = V / R
data.hint: What equation links voltage, current and resistance?

data.question: A 12 V cell is connected in a series loop with a 4 Ω resistor. What is the current in the circuit?
workingSteps: Apply Ohm's law: V = I × R, so I = V / R
data.hint: What equation links voltage, current and resistance?

data.question: A 9 V cell is connected in a series loop with a 15 Ω resistor. What is the current in the circuit?
workingSteps: Apply Ohm's law: V = I × R, so I = V / R
data.hint: What equation links voltage, current and resistance?

data.question: A 230 V cell is connected in a series loop with a 46 Ω resistor. What is the current in the circuit?
workingSteps: Apply Ohm's law: V = I × R, so I = V / R
data.hint: What equation links voltage, current and resistance?

data.question: A 6 V battery is connected in series with a 2 Ω resistor and a 4 Ω resistor. What is the p.d. across the 2 Ω resistor?
data.hint: In a series circuit the current is the same everywhere — find it first.

data.question: A 12 V battery is connected in series with a 3 Ω resistor and a 9 Ω resistor. What is the p.d. across the 3 Ω resistor?
data.hint: In a series circuit the current is the same everywhere — find it first.

data.question: A 9 V battery is connected in series with a 5 Ω resistor and a 10 Ω resistor. What is the p.d. across the 5 Ω resistor?
data.hint: In a series circuit the current is the same everywhere — find it first.

data.question: A 6 V cell is connected across two parallel branches: one with a 2 Ω resistor, the other with a 3 Ω resistor. What is the total current drawn from the cell?
workingSteps: In parallel, the p.d. across each branch equals the supply p.d.
data.hint: What stays the same across each branch of a parallel circuit?

data.question: A 12 V cell is connected across two parallel branches: one with a 4 Ω resistor, the other with a 6 Ω resistor. What is the total current drawn from the cell?
workingSteps: In parallel, the p.d. across each branch equals the supply p.d.
data.hint: What stays the same across each branch of a parallel circuit?

data.question: A 9 V cell is connected across two parallel branches: one with a 10 Ω resistor, the other with a 15 Ω resistor. What is the total current drawn from the cell?
workingSteps: In parallel, the p.d. across each branch equals the supply p.d.
data.hint: What stays the same across each branch of a parallel circuit?

data.narrative: You want to measure the current through the bulb AND the potential difference across it at the same time.
data.challenge: Place the ammeter and the voltmeter correctly on the circuit.

data.narrative: Two bulbs in series with a cell. When you close the switch neither bulb lights up.
data.challenge: Identify the most likely fault and test it in the simulator.

guidedQuestions: Build two identical bulbs in series. Then rebuild them in parallel. Which is brighter, and why?
What happens to the brightness of bulb A when you unscrew bulb B — in series, and in parallel?
discoveryGoals: Parallel branches share the supply p.d.; series branches share it.
Removing a bulb in series breaks the whole loop; in parallel, only that branch is affected.
